title: Diosma ternata
intro: Diosma ternata, also known as Cape buchu, is a plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the Rutaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and antimicrobial activity. The bioactive constituents of Diosma ternata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, particularly diosmetin and diosmin. Diosma ternata is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Diosma ternata is classified as a species within the family Rutaceae. This family includes plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. Diosma ternata is further classified as a member of the genus Diosma, which consists of shrubs native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. The genus Diosma is part of the order Sapindales. Diosma ternata is also known as rock-diosma.
